questionText: A sticker page had 12 equal parts. First, 3/12 of the page was covered with shiny star stickers. Then 6/12 more of the page was covered with animal stickers, so 9/12 of the page was filled. Later, some animal stickers were peeled off, but none of the star stickers were removed. After that, 4/12 of the page was still filled. How much of the animal sticker part was peeled off?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can add and subtract fractions with the same denominator
summary: MCQ for Grade 4 Fractions: Adding & Subtracting Fractions, aligned to Students can add and subtract fractions with the same denominator.
explanation: The star stickers still cover 3/12 because none of them were removed. Since 4/12 of the page is filled at the end, the animal stickers left must be 4/12 - 3/12 = 1/12. At first, the animal stickers covered 6/12, so the amount peeled off is 6/12 - 1/12 = 5/12. So 5/12 is correct. The option 4/12 is the total filled at the end, 3/12 is the star part, and 1/12 is the animal part left after peeling.
